1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# :markup: markdown
4	
5	require "action_view"
6	require "action_controller/log_subscriber"
7	require "action_controller/structured_event_subscriber"
8	require "action_controller/metal/params_wrapper"
9	
10	module ActionController
11	  # # Action Controller Base
12	  #
13	  # Action Controllers are the core of a web request in Rails. They are made up of
14	  # one or more actions that are executed on request and then either it renders a
15	  # template or redirects to another action. An action is defined as a public
16	  # method on the controller, which will automatically be made accessible to the
17	  # web-server through Rails Routes.
18	  #
19	  # By default, only the ApplicationController in a Rails application inherits
20	  # from `ActionController::Base`. All other controllers inherit from
21	  # ApplicationController. This gives you one class to configure things such as
22	  # request forgery protection and filtering of sensitive request parameters.
23	  #
24	  # A sample controller could look like this:
25	  #
26	  #     class PostsController < ApplicationController
27	  #       def index
28	  #         @posts = Post.all
29	  #       end
30	  #
31	  #       def create
32	  #         @post = Post.create params[:post]
33	  #         redirect_to posts_path
34	  #       end
35	  #     end
36	  #
37	  # Actions, by default, render a template in the `app/views` directory
38	  # corresponding to the name of the controller and action after executing code in
39	  # the action. For example, the `index` action of the PostsController would
40	  # render the template `app/views/posts/index.html.erb` by default after
41	  # populating the `@posts` instance variable.
42	  #
43	  # Unlike index, the create action will not render a template. After performing
44	  # its main purpose (creating a new post), it initiates a redirect instead. This
45	  # redirect works by returning an external `302 Moved` HTTP response that takes
46	  # the user to the index action.
47	  #
48	  # These two methods represent the two basic action archetypes used in Action
49	  # Controllers: Get-and-show and do-and-redirect. Most actions are variations on
50	  # these themes.
51	  #
52	  # ## Requests
53	  #
54	  # For every request, the router determines the value of the `controller` and
55	  # `action` keys. These determine which controller and action are called. The
56	  # remaining request parameters, the session (if one is available), and the full
57	  # request with all the HTTP headers are made available to the action through
58	  # accessor methods. Then the action is performed.
59	  #
60	  # The full request object is available via the request accessor and is primarily
61	  # used to query for HTTP headers:
62	  #
63	  #     def server_ip
64	  #       location = request.env["REMOTE_ADDR"]
65	  #       render plain: "This server hosted at #{location}"
66	  #     end
67	  #
68	  # ## Parameters
69	  #
70	  # All request parameters, whether they come from a query string in the URL or
71	  # form data submitted through a POST request are available through the `params`
72	  # method which returns a hash. For example, an action that was performed through
73	  # `/posts?category=All&limit=5` will include `{ "category" => "All", "limit" =>
74	  # "5" }` in `params`.
75	  #
76	  # It's also possible to construct multi-dimensional parameter hashes by
77	  # specifying keys using brackets, such as:
78	  #
79	  #     <input type="text" name="post[name]" value="david">
80	  #     <input type="text" name="post[address]" value="hyacintvej">
81	  #
82	  # A request coming from a form holding these inputs will include `{ "post" => {
83	  # "name" => "david", "address" => "hyacintvej" } }`. If the address input had
84	  # been named `post[address][street]`, the `params` would have included `{ "post"
85	  # => { "address" => { "street" => "hyacintvej" } } }`. There's no limit to the
86	  # depth of the nesting.
87	  #
88	  # ## Sessions
89	  #
90	  # Sessions allow you to store objects in between requests. This is useful for
91	  # objects that are not yet ready to be persisted, such as a Signup object
92	  # constructed in a multi-paged process, or objects that don't change much and
93	  # are needed all the time, such as a User object for a system that requires
94	  # login. The session should not be used, however, as a cache for objects where
95	  # it's likely they could be changed unknowingly. It's usually too much work to
96	  # keep it all synchronized -- something databases already excel at.
97	  #
98	  # You can place objects in the session by using the `session` method, which
99	  # accesses a hash:
100	  #
101	  #     session[:person] = Person.authenticate(user_name, password)
102	  #
103	  # You can retrieve it again through the same hash:
104	  #
105	  #     "Hello #{session[:person]}"
106	  #
107	  # For removing objects from the session, you can either assign a single key to
108	  # `nil`:
109	  #
110	  #     # removes :person from session
111	  #     session[:person] = nil
112	  #
113	  # or you can remove the entire session with `reset_session`.
114	  #
115	  # By default, sessions are stored in an encrypted browser cookie (see
116	  # ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore). Thus the user will not be able to read
117	  # or edit the session data. However, the user can keep a copy of the cookie even
118	  # after it has expired, so you should avoid storing sensitive information in
119	  # cookie-based sessions.
120	  #
121	  # ## Responses
122	  #
123	  # Each action results in a response, which holds the headers and document to be
124	  # sent to the user's browser. The actual response object is generated
125	  # automatically through the use of renders and redirects and requires no user
126	  # intervention.
127	  #
128	  # ## Renders
129	  #
130	  # Action Controller sends content to the user by using one of five rendering
131	  # methods. The most versatile and common is the rendering of a template.
132	  # Also included with \Rails is Action View, which enables rendering of ERB
133	  # templates. It's automatically configured. The controller passes objects to the
134	  # view by assigning instance variables:
135	  #
136	  #     def show
137	  #       @post = Post.find(params[:id])
138	  #     end
139	  #
140	  # Which are then automatically available to the view:
141	  #
142	  #     Title: <%= @post.title %>
143	  #
144	  # You don't have to rely on the automated rendering. For example, actions that
145	  # could result in the rendering of different templates will use the manual
146	  # rendering methods:
147	  #
148	  #     def search
149	  #       @results = Search.find(params[:query])
150	  #       case @results.count

Perfect! Now I have all the pieces. Let me create a comprehensive summary of the Rack entry point and middleware stack assembly in Rails.

## Summary: Rails Rack Entry Point and Middleware Stack Assembly

Based on my thorough examination of the Rails codebase, here's the complete trace of how `Rails::Application` becomes a Rack app and how middleware is assembled:

### 1. RAILS::APPLICATION BECOMES A RACK APP

**The Core Structure:**
- `Rails::Application` (railties/lib/rails/application.rb:63) inherits from `Rails::Engine` (railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:348)
- Both implement the Rack interface through the `call` method
- `Rails::Application` aliases `build_middleware_stack` to `app` (railties/lib/rails/application.rb:672)

**Key Methods:**

**Engine#call** - railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:532-535
```
def call(env)
  req = build_request env
  app.call req.env
end
```
This is the Rack entry point that processes incoming requests.

**Engine#app** - railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:515-523
```
def app
  @app || @app_build_lock.synchronize {
    @app ||= begin
      stack = default_middleware_stack
      config.middleware = build_middleware.merge_into(stack)
      config.middleware.build(endpoint)
    end
  }
end
```
This lazily builds and caches the Rack application by:
1. Creating the default middleware stack
2. Merging configured middleware into it
3. Building the middleware stack with an endpoint (the router)

**Engine#endpoint** - railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:527-529
```
def endpoint
  self.class.endpoint || routes
end
```
Returns the endpoint (defaults to routes, which is an `ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet`)

**Engine#build_request** - railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:771-777
```
def build_request(env)
  env.merge!(env_config)
  req = ActionDispatch::Request.new env
  req.routes = routes
  req.engine_script_name = req.script_name
  req
end
```

**Application overrides:**

**Application#build_middleware** - railties/lib/rails/application.rb:770-772
```
def build_middleware
  config.app_middleware + super
end
```
Combines application-level middleware (from `config/initializers` and application config) with engine middleware.

**Application#build_request** - railties/lib/rails/application.rb:763-768
Adds `ORIGINAL_FULLPATH` and `ORIGINAL_SCRIPT_NAME` to the Rack env.

**Application#default_middleware_stack** - railties/lib/rails/application.rb:740-743
```
def default_middleware_stack
  default_stack = DefaultMiddlewareStack.new(self, config, paths)
  default_stack.build_stack
end
```

---

### 2. DEFAULT MIDDLEWARE STACK ASSEMBLY

**DefaultMiddlewareStack** - railties/lib/rails/application/default_middleware_stack.rb

The `build_stack` method (lines 14-112) constructs the middleware stack in this order:

1. **ActionDispatch::HostAuthorization** (if hosts configured) - line 17
2. **ActionDispatch::AssumeSSL** (if assume_ssl) - line 21
3. **ActionDispatch::SSL** (if force_ssl) - line 25
4. **Rack::Sendfile** (if x_sendfile_header configured) - line 30
5. **ActionDispatch::Static** (if public_file_server enabled) - line 36
6. **Rack::Cache** (if rack_cache configured) - line 41
7. **Rack::Lock** (if concurrency disabled) - line 48
8. **ActionDispatch::Executor** (app.executor) - line 51
9. **ActionDispatch::ServerTiming** (if server_timing) - line 53
10. **Rack::Runtime** - line 54
11. **Rack::MethodOverride** (unless api_only) - line 55
12. **ActionDispatch::RequestId** - line 56
13. **ActionDispatch::RemoteIp** - line 57
14. **Rails::Rack::SilenceRequest** (if silence_healthcheck_path) - line 60
15. **Rails::Rack::Logger** - line 63
16. **ActionDispatch::ShowExceptions** - line 64
17. **ActionDispatch::DebugExceptions** - line 65
18. **ActionDispatch::ActionableExceptions** (if consider_all_requests_local) - line 68
19. **ActionDispatch::Reloader** (if reloading_enabled) - line 72
20. **ActionDispatch::Callbacks** - line 75
21. **ActionDispatch::Cookies** (unless api_only) - line 76
22. **Session Store** (if configured and not api_only) - line 82
23. **ActionDispatch::Flash** (unless api_only) - line 86
24. **ActionDispatch::ContentSecurityPolicy::Middleware** (unless api_only) - line 87
25. **ActionDispatch::PermissionsPolicy::Middleware** (if permissions_policy) - line 88
26. **Rack::Head** - line 91
27. **Rack::ConditionalGet** - line 92
28. **Rack::ETag** - line 93
29. **Rack::TempfileReaper** (unless api_only) - line 95
30. **ActiveRecord::Middleware::DatabaseSelector** (if active_record configured) - line 102
31. **ActiveRecord::Middleware::ShardSelector** (if shard_resolver configured) - line 108

---

### 3. MIDDLEWARE STACK IMPLEMENTATION

**ActionDispatch::MiddlewareStack** - actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/middleware/stack.rb

**Initialization** (lines 76-79):
```
def initialize(*args)
  @middlewares = []
  yield(self) if block_given?
end
```

**Key Methods:**

**use** - lines 161-163: Appends middleware to the stack
**build** - lines 166-174: Constructs the nested Rack app
```
def build(app = nil, &block)
  instrumenting = ActiveSupport::Notifications.notifier.listening?(InstrumentationProxy::EVENT_NAME)
  middlewares.freeze.reverse.inject(app || block) do |a, e|
    if instrumenting
      e.build_instrumented(a)
    else
      e.build(a)
    end
  end
end
```
Builds a nested chain by:
1. Reversing the middleware array
2. Injecting (folding) each middleware into the previous one
3. Each middleware wraps the previous app

**Middleware#build** - lines 43-45: Creates a middleware instance
```
def build(app)
  klass.new(app, *args, &block)
end
```

**MiddlewareStackProxy** - railties/lib/rails/configuration.rb

Allows deferred middleware configuration via `config.middleware`:

**merge_into** - lines 88-94: Applies all queued middleware operations to a stack
```
def merge_into(other)
  (@operations + @delete_operations).each do |operation|
    operation.call(other)
  end
  other
end
```

Operations include:
- `use(klass, *args)` - appends middleware
- `insert_before(index, klass, *args)` - inserts before
- `insert_after(index, klass, *args)` - inserts after
- `swap(target, klass, *args)` - replaces middleware
- `delete(target)` - removes middleware
- `move_before(target, source)` - moves middleware before
- `move_after(target, source)` - moves middleware after

---

### 4. REQUEST CALL CHAIN EXECUTION

**Request Processing Flow:**

1. **Entry: Rails.application.call(env)** → Engine#call (railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:532)
   - Merges env_config into env
   - Creates ActionDispatch::Request object
   - Calls app.call(req.env)

2. **Middleware Stack Execution** → MiddlewareStack#build result
   - Each middleware receives the previous app and wraps it
   - Request flows through middleware layers in order
   - Each middleware can process request before/after the next middleware

3. **Endpoint/Router: ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet#call** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:903-907
   ```
   def call(env)
     req = make_request(env)
     req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)
     @router.serve(req)
   end
   ```

4. **Journey Router: Router#serve** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb:30-41
   - Calls `recognize(req)` to match routes
   - Extracts path parameters
   - Calls `route.app.serve(req)` (typically ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet::Dispatcher)

5. **Route Dispatcher** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:39-67
   - Instantiates controller
   - Dispatches to action
   - Returns response

6. **Middleware Response Handling**
   - Response bubbles back through middleware in reverse order
   - Each middleware can modify the response
   - Final response returned to Rack server

---

### KEY FILES AND LINE NUMBERS

| Component | File Path | Key Lines |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| Application class | railties/lib/rails/application.rb | 63, 672, 740, 770-772, 763 |
| Engine class (Rack interface) | railties/lib/rails/engine.rb | 348, 515-523, 532-535, 527-529, 771-777, 779-781 |
| Finisher initializers | railties/lib/rails/application/finisher.rb | 56-58 (build_middleware_stack init) |
| Default middleware stack | railties/lib/rails/application/default_middleware_stack.rb | 8-12, 14-112 |
| MiddlewareStack | actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/middleware/stack.rb | 14-194, 166-174 (build method) |
| MiddlewareStackProxy | railties/lib/rails/configuration.rb | 46-102, 88-94 (merge_into) |
| RouteSet#call | actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb | 903-907 |
| Journey Router#serve | actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb | 30-41 |
| Route Dispatcher | actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb | 39-67 |

### 3. MIDDLEWARE STACK IMPLEMENTATION

**ActionDispatch::MiddlewareStack** - actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/middleware/stack.rb

**Initialization** (lines 76-79):
```
def initialize(*args)
  @middlewares = []
  yield(self) if block_given?
end
```

**Key Methods:**

**use** - lines 161-163: Appends middleware to the stack
**build** - lines 166-174: Constructs the nested Rack app
```
def build(app = nil, &block)
  instrumenting = ActiveSupport::Notifications.notifier.listening?(InstrumentationProxy::EVENT_NAME)
  middlewares.freeze.reverse.inject(app || block) do |a, e|
    if instrumenting
      e.build_instrumented(a)
    else
      e.build(a)
    end
  end
end
```
Builds a nested chain by:
1. Reversing the middleware array
2. Injecting (folding) each middleware into the previous one
3. Each middleware wraps the previous app

**Middleware#build** - lines 43-45: Creates a middleware instance
```
def build(app)
  klass.new(app, *args, &block)
end
```

**MiddlewareStackProxy** - railties/lib/rails/configuration.rb

Allows deferred middleware configuration via `config.middleware`:

**merge_into** - lines 88-94: Applies all queued middleware operations to a stack
```
def merge_into(other)
  (@operations + @delete_operations).each do |operation|
    operation.call(other)
  end
  other
end
```

Operations include:
- `use(klass, *args)` - appends middleware
- `insert_before(index, klass, *args)` - inserts before
- `insert_after(index, klass, *args)` - inserts after
- `swap(target, klass, *args)` - replaces middleware
- `delete(target)` - removes middleware
- `move_before(target, source)` - moves middleware before
- `move_after(target, source)` - moves middleware after

---

### 4. REQUEST CALL CHAIN EXECUTION

**Request Processing Flow:**

1. **Entry: Rails.application.call(env)** → Engine#call (railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:532)
   - Merges env_config into env
   - Creates ActionDispatch::Request object
   - Calls app.call(req.env)

2. **Middleware Stack Execution** → MiddlewareStack#build result
   - Each middleware receives the previous app and wraps it
   - Request flows through middleware layers in order
   - Each middleware can process request before/after the next middleware

3. **Endpoint/Router: ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet#call** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:903-907
   ```
   def call(env)
     req = make_request(env)
     req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)
     @router.serve(req)
   end
   ```

4. **Journey Router: Router#serve** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb:30-41
   - Calls `recognize(req)` to match routes
   - Extracts path parameters
   - Calls `route.app.serve(req)` (typically ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet::Dispatcher)

5. **Route Dispatcher** → actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:39-67
   - Instantiates controller
   - Dispatches to action
   - Returns response

6. **Middleware Response Handling**
   - Response bubbles back through middleware in reverse order
   - Each middleware can modify the response
   - Final response returned to Rack server

### 6. ENV TRANSFORMATION FLOW

**On the way IN (request → app):**
1. Rack middleware stack receives `env` hash from web server
2. Each middleware **modifies `env`** before passing to next:
   - **RequestId**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.request_id"]` + response header
   - **Static**: May modify `env["PATH_INFO"]` if serving precompressed files
   - **Cookies**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.cookies"]` (cookie jar instance)
   - **Session**: Sets `env["rack.session"]` from cookie store's `load_session`
   - **RemoteIp**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.remote_ip"]`
3. **ActionDispatch::Request wraps env** — inherits from Rack::Request, provides high-level accessors:
   - Lazily reads from env keys
   - Can write back to env for downstream middleware
4. **Router's `call(env)`** creates final Request object and routes to controller

**On the way OUT (response → client):**
1. Controller action returns (or renders), populates **Response object**
2. Response object **built during rendering**:
   - Body written to `@stream` via `response.write` or `response.body =`
   - Headers set via `response.headers` or `response[key] =`
3. **`response.to_a`** converts to Rack triplet (called during response lifecycle):
   - Calls `commit!` → `before_committed` → finalizes headers/cache/body
   - **`before_sending`** called by response lifecycle → **`request.commit_cookie_jar!`** persists cookies + session to Set-Cookie
   - Returns `[status, headers, RackBody.new(self)]`
4. **Middleware stack unwinds**, each middleware can **transform the [status, headers, body] response array**
5. **Final response sent to client**

---

### KEY ENV KEYS SET BY MIDDLEWARE

| Key | Set By | Value |
|-----|--------|-------|
| `action_dispatch.request_id` | RequestId | UUID or X-Request-Id header (line 340 in request.rb) |
| `action_dispatch.remote_ip` | RemoteIp | Detected/trusted IP address |
| `action_dispatch.cookies` | Cookies | CookieJar instance |
| `action_dispatch.request.unsigned_session_cookie` | CookieStore | Decrypted session data hash |
| `rack.session` | Session middleware | Session hash for request |
| `rack.session.options` | Session middleware | Session options (ID, etc.) |
| `action_dispatch.request.flash_hash` | Flash::RequestMethods | FlashHash instance |
| `action_dispatch.route` | Router | Matched Route object |
| `action_dispatch.routes` | Router | RouteSet instance |
| `action_controller.instance` | Controller | Controller instance |

---

This mapping shows the complete request/response lifecycle: **env dict → middleware transforms → Request wrapper → Controller action → Response building → to_a conversion → Middleware unwinding → Response sent**.

- **`Request::Session.set(req, session)`** - Line 39-41: Sets `rack.session` in env
- **`default_session`** - Line 525-527 in request.rb: Returns disabled session for API-only apps

**Session lazy-load via mixin:**
- **`Request::Session#initialize(by, req, enabled:)`** - `/home/bench/repos/rails/actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/request/session.rb` Line 76-85: Stores session store reference and request
- **`Request::Session#load_for_read!`** - (line 115 in session.rb): Calls `@by.send(:load_session, @req)` on first access

---

### 4. RESPONSE GENERATION (ActionDispatch::Response)

File: `/home/bench/repos/rails/actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/http/response.rb`

**Initialization & building:**
- **`initialize(status = 200, headers = nil, body = [])`** - Line 198-217: Initializes `@headers`, sets body/status, initializes concurrency monitoring

**Headers & body setters:**
- **`body=(body)`** - Line 385-398: Wraps body in `Buffer` if string/array, or stores as-is
- **`status=(status)`** - Line 274-276: Sets `@status` via `Response.rack_status_code(status)`

**Rack conversion:**
- **`to_a`** - Line 461-464: Calls `commit!` then returns `rack_response(@status, @headers.to_hash)` — **converts Response to [status, headers, body] array**
- **`rack_response(status, headers)`** - Line 595-601: Returns `[status, headers, []]` for no-content responses or `[status, headers, RackBody.new(self)]` for others
- **`RackBody` inner class** - Line 544-586: Wraps response stream, delegates `to_ary`, `each`, `call`, `to_path` to underlying stream

**Lifecycle hooks:**
- **`before_committed`** - Line 514-520: Sets default content type, merges cache control, handles conditional GET/no-content
- **`before_sending`** - Line 522-530: Final commit, calls `@request.commit_cookie_jar!` for session persistence
- **`commit!`** - Line 234-240: Calls `before_committed`, sets `@committed = true`, broadcasts condition

---

### 5. ROUTER (ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet)

File: `/home/bench/repos/rails/actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb`

**Main middleware entry point:**
- **`call(env)`** - Line 903-907: 
  1. Creates request: `req = make_request(env)`
  2. Normalizes path: `req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)`
  3. Routes request: `@router.serve(req)` — returns response array `[status, headers, body]`

**Request creation:**
- **`make_request(env)`** - Line 433-435: Returns `ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)`

**Dispatcher execution:**
- **`Dispatcher#serve(req)`** - Line 46-57: Extracts `params`, gets `controller_class`, creates response object with `controller.make_response!(req)`, calls `dispatch(controller, action, req, res)`

---

### 6. ENV TRANSFORMATION FLOW

**On the way IN (request → app):**
1. Rack middleware stack receives `env` hash from web server
2. Each middleware **modifies `env`** before passing to next:
   - **RequestId**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.request_id"]` + response header
   - **Static**: May modify `env["PATH_INFO"]` if serving precompressed files
   - **Cookies**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.cookies"]` (cookie jar instance)
   - **Session**: Sets `env["rack.session"]` from cookie store's `load_session`
   - **RemoteIp**: Sets `env["action_dispatch.remote_ip"]`
3. **ActionDispatch::Request wraps env** — inherits from Rack::Request, provides high-level accessors:
   - Lazily reads from env keys
   - Can write back to env for downstream middleware
4. **Router's `call(env)`** creates final Request object and routes to controller

**On the way OUT (response → client):**
1. Controller action returns (or renders), populates **Response object**
2. Response object **built during rendering**:
   - Body written to `@stream` via `response.write` or `response.body =`
   - Headers set via `response.headers` or `response[key] =`
3. **`response.to_a`** converts to Rack triplet (called during response lifecycle):
   - Calls `commit!` → `before_committed` → finalizes headers/cache/body
   - **`before_sending`** called by response lifecycle → **`request.commit_cookie_jar!`** persists cookies + session to Set-Cookie
   - Returns `[status, headers, RackBody.new(self)]`
4. **Middleware stack unwinds**, each middleware can **transform the [status, headers, body] response array**
5. **Final response sent to client**

---

### KEY ENV KEYS SET BY MIDDLEWARE

| Key | Set By | Value |
|-----|--------|-------|
| `action_dispatch.request_id` | RequestId | UUID or X-Request-Id header (line 340 in request.rb) |
| `action_dispatch.remote_ip` | RemoteIp | Detected/trusted IP address |
| `action_dispatch.cookies` | Cookies | CookieJar instance |
| `action_dispatch.request.unsigned_session_cookie` | CookieStore | Decrypted session data hash |
| `rack.session` | Session middleware | Session hash for request |
| `rack.session.options` | Session middleware | Session options (ID, etc.) |
| `action_dispatch.request.flash_hash` | Flash::RequestMethods | FlashHash instance |
| `action_dispatch.route` | Router | Matched Route object |
| `action_dispatch.routes` | Router | RouteSet instance |
| `action_controller.instance` | Controller | Controller instance |

---

This mapping shows the complete request/response lifecycle: **env dict → middleware transforms → Request wrapper → Controller action → Response building → to_a conversion → Middleware unwinding → Response sent**.

def helpers_paths
      paths["app/helpers"].existent
    end

    # Returns the underlying Rack application for this engine.
    def app
      @app || @app_build_lock.synchronize {
        @app ||= begin
          stack = default_middleware_stack
          config.middleware = build_middleware.merge_into(stack)
          config.middleware.build(endpoint)
        end
      }
    end

    # Returns the endpoint for this engine. If none is registered,
    # defaults to an ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet.
    def endpoint
      self.class.endpoint || routes
    end

    # Define the Rack API for this engine.
    def call(env)
      req = build_request env
      app.call req.env
    end

    # Defines additional Rack env configuration that is added on each call.
    def env_config
      @env_config ||= {}

file.content:
def performed?
      response_body || response.committed?
    end

    def dispatch(name, request, response) # :nodoc:
      set_request!(request)
      set_response!(response)
      process(name)
      request.commit_flash
      to_a
    end

    def set_response!(response) # :nodoc:
      if @_response
        _, _, body = @_response
        body.close if body.respond_to?(:close)
      end

      @_response = response
    end

    # Assign the response and mark it as committed. No further processing will
    # occur.
    def response=(response)
      set_response!(response)

      # Force `performed?` to return true:
      @_response_body = true
    end

    def set_request!(request) # :nodoc:
      @_request = request
      @_request.controller_instance = self
    end

    def to_a # :nodoc:
      response.to_a
    end

    def reset_session
      @_request.reset_session
    end

    class_attribute :middleware_stack, default: ActionController::MiddlewareStack.new

    class << self
      # Pushes the given Rack middleware and its arguments to the bottom of the
      # middleware stack.
      def use(...)
        middleware_stack.use(...)
      end
    end

    # The middleware stack used by this controller.
    #
    # By default uses a variation of ActionDispatch::MiddlewareStack which allows
    # for the following syntax:
    #
    #     class PostsController < ApplicationController
    #       use AuthenticationMiddleware, except: [:index, :show]
    #     end
    #
    # Read more about [Rails middleware stack]
    # (https://guides.rubyonrails.org/rails_on_rack.html#action-dispatcher-middleware-stack)
    # in the guides.
    def self.middleware
      middleware_stack
    end

    # Returns a Rack endpoint for the given action name.
    def self.action(name)
      app = lambda { |env|
        req = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
        res = make_response! req
        new.dispatch(name, req, res)
      }

      if middleware_stack.any?
        middleware_stack.build(name, app)
      else
        app
      end
    end

    # Direct dispatch to the controller. Instantiates the controller, then executes
    # the action named `name`.
    def self.dispatch(name, req, res)
      if middleware_stack.any?
        middleware_stack.build(name) { |env| new.dispatch(name, req, res) }.call req.env
      else
        new.dispatch(name, req, res)
      end
    end
  end
end

# The actual method that is called is determined by calling #method_for_action.
    # If no method can handle the action, then an AbstractController::ActionNotFound
    # error is raised.
    def process(action, ...)
      @_action_name = action.to_s

      unless action_name = _find_action_name(@_action_name)
        raise ActionNotFound.new("The action '#{action}' could not be found for #{self.class.name}", self, action)
      end

      @_response_body = nil

      process_action(action_name, ...)
    end

    # Delegates to the class's ::controller_path.
    def controller_path
      self.class.controller_path
    end

    # Delegates to the class's ::action_methods.
    def action_methods
      self.class.action_methods
    end

    # Returns true if a method for the action is available and can be dispatched,
    # false otherwise.
    #
    # Notice that `action_methods.include?("foo")` may return false and
    # `available_action?("foo")` returns true because this method considers actions
    # that are also available through other means, for example, implicit render
    # ones.
    #
    # #### Parameters
    # *   `action_name` - The name of an action to be tested
    #
    def available_action?(action_name)
      _find_action_name(action_name)
    end

end
    end

    private
      def process_action(*)
        ActiveSupport::ExecutionContext[:controller] = self

        raw_payload = {
          controller: self.class.name,
          action: action_name,
          request: request,
          params: request.filtered_parameters,
          headers: request.headers,
          format: request.format.ref,
          method: request.request_method,
          path: request.filtered_path
        }

        ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("start_processing.action_controller", raw_payload)

        ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("process_action.action_controller", raw_payload) do |payload|
          result = super
          payload[:response] = response
          payload[:status]   = response.status
          result
        rescue => error
          payload[:status] = ActionDispatch::ExceptionWrapper.status_code_for_exception(error.class.name)
          raise
        ensure
          append_info_to_payload(payload)
        end
      end

      # A hook invoked every time a before callback is halted.
      def halted_callback_hook(filter, _)
        ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("halted_callback.action_controller", filter: filter)
      end

      # A hook which allows you to clean up any time, wrongly taken into account in
      # views, like database querying time.

file.content:
# Lifespan: Per controller
      @_view_renderer ||= ActionView::Renderer.new(lookup_context)
    end

    def render_to_body(options = {}, &block)
      _process_options(options)
      _process_render_template_options(options)
      _render_template(options, &block)
    end

    private
      # Find and render a template based on the options given.
      def _render_template(options, &block)
        variant = options.delete(:variant)
        assigns = options.delete(:assigns)
        context = view_context

        context.assign assigns if assigns
        lookup_context.variants = variant if variant

        rendered_template = context.in_rendering_context(options) do |renderer|
          renderer.render_to_object(context, options, &block)
        end

        rendered_format = rendered_template.format || lookup_context.formats.first
        @_rendered_format = Template::Types[rendered_format]

        rendered_template.body
      end

      # Assign the rendered format to look up context.
      def _process_format(format)
        super
        lookup_context.formats = [format.to_sym] if format.to_sym
      end

end

      def serve(req)
        recognize(req) do |route, parameters|
          req.path_parameters = parameters
          req.route = route

          _, headers, _ = response = route.app.serve(req)

          return response unless headers[Constants::X_CASCADE] == "pass"
        end

        [404, { Constants::X_CASCADE => "pass" }, ["Not Found"]]
      end

      def recognize(req, &block)
        req_params  = req.path_parameters
        path_info   = req.path_info
        script_name = req.script_name

        routes = filter_routes(path_info)

        custom_routes.each { |r|
          routes << r if r.path.match?(path_info)
        }

file.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

module ActionView
  class TemplateRenderer < AbstractRenderer # :nodoc:
    def render(context, options, &block)
      @details = extract_details(options)
      template = determine_template(options, &block)

      prepend_formats(template.format)

      render_template(context, template, options[:layout], options[:locals] || {})
    end

    private
      # Determine the template to be rendered using the given options.
      def determine_template(options, &block)
        keys = options.has_key?(:locals) ? options[:locals].keys : []

        if options.key?(:body)
          Template::Text.new(options[:body])
        elsif options.key?(:plain)
          Template::Text.new(options[:plain])
        elsif options.key?(:html)
          Template::HTML.new(options[:html], formats.first)
        elsif options.key?(:file)
          if File.exist?(options[:file])
            Template::RawFile.new(options[:file])
          else
            if Pathname.new(options[:file]).absolute?
              raise ArgumentError, "File #{options[:file]} does not exist"
            else
              raise ArgumentError, "`render file:` should be given the absolute path to a file. '#{options[:file]}' was given instead"
            end
          end
        elsif options.key?(:inline)
          handler = Template.handler_for_extension(options[:type] || "erb")
          format = if handler.respond_to?(:default_format)
            handler.default_format
          else
            @lookup_context.formats.first
          end
          Template::Inline.new(options[:inline], "inline template", handler, locals: keys, format: format)
        elsif options.key?(:renderable)
          Template::Renderable.new(options[:renderable], &block)
        elsif options.key?(:template)
          if options[:template].respond_to?(:render)
            options[:template]
          else
            @lookup_context.find_template(options[:template], options[:prefixes], false, keys, @details)
          end
        else
          raise ArgumentError, "You invoked render but did not give any of :body, :file, :html, :inline, :partial, :plain, :renderable, or :template option."
        end
      end

      # Renders the given template. A string representing the layout can be
      # supplied as well.
      def render_template(view, template, layout_name, locals)
        render_with_layout(view, template, layout_name, locals) do |layout|
          ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument(
            "render_template.action_view",
            identifier: template.identifier,
            layout: layout && layout.virtual_path,
            locals: locals
          ) do
            template.render(view, locals) { |*name| view._layout_for(*name) }
          end
        end
      end

      def render_with_layout(view, template, path, locals)
        layout  = path && find_layout(path, locals.keys, [formats.first])

        body = if layout
          ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("render_layout.action_view", identifier: layout.identifier) do
            view.view_flow.set(:layout, yield(layout))
            layout.render(view, locals) { |*name| view._layout_for(*name) }
          end
        else
          yield
        end
        build_rendered_template(body, template)
      end

      # This is the method which actually finds the layout using details in the lookup
      # context object. If no layout is found, it checks if at least a layout with
      # the given name exists across all details before raising the error.
      def find_layout(layout, keys, formats)
        resolve_layout(layout, keys, formats)
      end

# we use a bang in this instrumentation because you don't want to
    # consume this in production. This is only slow if it's being listened to.
    def render(view, locals, buffer = nil, implicit_locals: [], add_to_stack: true, &block)
      instrument_render_template do
        compile!(view)

        if strict_locals? && @strict_local_keys && !implicit_locals.empty?
          locals_to_ignore = implicit_locals - @strict_local_keys
          locals.except!(*locals_to_ignore)
        end

        if buffer
          view._run(method_name, self, locals, buffer, add_to_stack: add_to_stack, has_strict_locals: strict_locals?, &block)
          nil
        else
          result = view._run(method_name, self, locals, OutputBuffer.new, add_to_stack: add_to_stack, has_strict_locals: strict_locals?, &block)
          result.is_a?(OutputBuffer) ? result.to_s : result
        end
      end
    rescue => e
      handle_render_error(view, e)
    end

    def type
      @type ||= Types[format]
    end

    def short_identifier
      @short_identifier ||= defined?(Rails.root) ? identifier.delete_prefix("#{Rails.root}/") : identifier
    end

# Compile a template. This method ensures a template is compiled
      # just once and removes the source after it is compiled.
      def compile!(view)
        return if @compiled

        # Templates can be used concurrently in threaded environments
        # so compilation and any instance variable modification must
        # be synchronized
        @compile_mutex.synchronize do
          # Any thread holding this lock will be compiling the template needed
          # by the threads waiting. So re-check the @compiled flag to avoid
          # re-compilation
          return if @compiled

          mod = view.compiled_method_container

          instrument("!compile_template") do
            compile(mod)
          end

          @compiled = true
        end
      end

      # This method compiles the source of the template. The compilation of templates
      # involves setting strict_locals! if applicable, encoding the template, and setting
      # frozen string literal.
      def compiled_source
        set_strict_locals = strict_locals!

Here's my outline:

1. **Rack entry point** — Rails::Engine#call
2. **Middleware stack assembly** — DefaultMiddlewareStack, MiddlewareStack#build
3. **Middleware execution** (30 layers, key ones explained)
4. **Routing** — RouteSet#call, Journey::Router#serve, #recognize, Dispatcher#serve
5. **Controller dispatch** — Metal.dispatch (class), Metal#dispatch (instance)
6. **Process pipeline** — AbstractController::Base#process, which orchestrates the entire action lifecycle
7. **Instrumentation and rescue layers** — where performance monitoring and error handling wrap the action execution
8. **Callbacks and action execution** — running before/after filters and then invoking the actual action method
9. **Rendering pipeline** — from the controller's render call through ActionView's template compilation and output generation
10. **Template lookup and compilation** — resolving the template file and compiling it into executable code
11. **Layout wrapping** — applying the layout template around the rendered action view
12. **Response finalization** — building the HTTP response with status, headers, and body
13. **Middleware unwind** — the response bubbles back through the middleware stack and returns as a Rack-compliant array to the server

content.text:
All line numbers confirmed. Here is the complete architectural walkthrough.

---

## Rails Full Request Lifecycle: Rack Entry to Rendered Response

---

## 1. Rack Entry Point

Every HTTP request enters at `Rails::Engine#call` — `Rails::Application` inherits this from `Engine`.

```
railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:532-535
```
```ruby
def call(env)
  req = build_request env    # wraps env in ActionDispatch::Request, merges env_config
  app.call req.env
end
```

`app` is built lazily on first request:

```
railties/lib/rails/engine.rb:515-523
```
```ruby
def app
  @app || @app_build_lock.synchronize {
    @app ||= begin
      stack = default_middleware_stack          # builds DefaultMiddlewareStack
      config.middleware = build_middleware.merge_into(stack)
      config.middleware.build(endpoint)         # endpoint = RouteSet
    end
  }
end
```

`MiddlewareStack#build` (actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/middleware/stack.rb:166-174) folds the stack into a nested chain with `inject`:

```ruby
middlewares.freeze.reverse.inject(app || block) do |a, e|
  e.build_instrumented(a)  # or e.build(a) if not instrumented
end
```

Each middleware's initializer receives the next app, so the whole stack is a chain of closures around `RouteSet`.

---

## 2. Middleware Stack (30 layers, in order)

Assembled by `DefaultMiddlewareStack#build_stack` at `railties/lib/rails/application/default_middleware_stack.rb:14-110`.

| # | Middleware | Condition | File |
|---|-----------|-----------|------|
| 1 | `ActionDispatch::HostAuthorization` | unless hosts empty | middleware/host_authorization.rb |
| 2 | `ActionDispatch::AssumeSSL` | if assume_ssl | middleware/assume_ssl.rb |
| 3 | `ActionDispatch::SSL` | if force_ssl | middleware/ssl.rb |
| 4 | `Rack::Sendfile` | if x_sendfile_header | (rack gem) |
| 5 | `ActionDispatch::Static` | if public_file_server.enabled | middleware/static.rb |
| 6 | `Rack::Cache` | if rack_cache configured | (rack-cache gem) |
| 7 | `Rack::Lock` | if allow_concurrency == false | (rack gem) |
| 8 | **`ActionDispatch::Executor`** | always | middleware/executor.rb |
| 9 | `ActionDispatch::ServerTiming` | if server_timing | middleware/server_timing.rb |
| 10 | `Rack::Runtime` | always | (rack gem) |
| 11 | `Rack::MethodOverride` | unless api_only | (rack gem) |
| 12 | **`ActionDispatch::RequestId`** | always | middleware/request_id.rb |
| 13 | `ActionDispatch::RemoteIp` | always | middleware/remote_ip.rb |
| 14 | `Rails::Rack::SilenceRequest` | if silence_healthcheck_path | rack/silence_request.rb |
| 15 | **`Rails::Rack::Logger`** | always | rack/logger.rb |
| 16 | `ActionDispatch::ShowExceptions` | always | middleware/show_exceptions.rb |
| 17 | `ActionDispatch::DebugExceptions` | always | middleware/debug_exceptions.rb |
| 18 | `ActionDispatch::ActionableExceptions` | if consider_all_requests_local | middleware/actionable_exceptions.rb |
| 19 | `ActionDispatch::Reloader` | if reloading_enabled? | middleware/reloader.rb |
| 20 | `ActionDispatch::Callbacks` | always | middleware/callbacks.rb |
| 21 | **`ActionDispatch::Cookies`** | unless api_only | middleware/cookies.rb |
| 22 | **Session store** (e.g. `CookieStore`) | unless api_only + session configured | middleware/session/cookie_store.rb |
| 23 | **`ActionDispatch::Flash`** | unless api_only | middleware/flash.rb |
| 24 | `ActionDispatch::ContentSecurityPolicy::Middleware` | unless api_only | middleware/content_security_policy.rb |
| 25 | `ActionDispatch::PermissionsPolicy::Middleware` | if permissions_policy | middleware/permissions_policy.rb |
| 26 | `Rack::Head` | always | (rack gem) |
| 27 | `Rack::ConditionalGet` | always | (rack gem) |
| 28 | `Rack::ETag` | always | (rack gem) |
| 29 | `Rack::TempfileReaper` | unless api_only | (rack gem) |
| 30 | `ActiveRecord::Middleware::DatabaseSelector` | if active_record db_selector | activerecord/lib/active_record/middleware/database_selector.rb |

**Key middleware behavior:**

`ActionDispatch::Executor#call` (middleware/executor.rb:13-55) wraps every request in `Rails.application.executor.run!(reset: true)`, which resets thread-local state (autoload, current attributes, execution context) and ensures `ActiveRecord` connections are returned to the pool even on streaming responses via `Rack::BodyProxy`.

`ActionDispatch::RequestId#call` stamps `env["action_dispatch.request_id"]` from `X-Request-Id` header or generates a UUID.

`ActionDispatch::Cookies` attaches a `CookieJar` at `env["action_dispatch.cookies"]`; `Session::CookieStore` deserializes the encrypted session cookie into `env["rack.session"]`.

`ActionDispatch::Flash` makes `FlashHash` available via `req.flash`; `commit_flash` is called on the way back out (metal.rb:253).

---

## 3. Routing

After passing through all middleware, the request reaches `ActionDispatch::Routing::RouteSet#call`:

```
actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:903-907
```
```ruby
def call(env)
  req = make_request(env)                                          # ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
  req.path_info = Journey::Router::Utils.normalize_path(req.path_info)
  @router.serve(req)
end
```

`@router` is an `ActionDispatch::Journey::Router`.

### 3a. Journey::Router#serve

```
actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/journey/router.rb:30-41
```
```ruby
def serve(req)
  recognize(req) do |route, parameters|
    req.path_parameters = parameters   # merges { controller:, action:, id:, … } into env
    req.route = route

    _, headers, _ = response = route.app.serve(req)

    return response unless headers[Constants::X_CASCADE] == "pass"
  end
  [404, { Constants::X_CASCADE => "pass" }, ["Not Found"]]
end
```

`recognize` (router.rb:43) filters routes by a compiled NFA/regex simulator (`filter_routes`), checks custom constraints, sorts by precedence, extracts named captures from the path pattern, and yields each candidate.

`route.app` is either `RouteSet::Dispatcher` (string routes like `"posts#show"`) or `RouteSet::StaticDispatcher` (lambda routes like `to: PostsController.action(:show)`).

### 3b. RouteSet::Dispatcher#serve

```
actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/routing/route_set.rb:46-57
```
```ruby
def serve(req)
  params     = req.path_parameters
  controller = controller req                   # → req.controller_class
  res        = controller.make_response! req    # builds ActionDispatch::Response
  dispatch(controller, params[:action], req, res)
end
```

`req.controller_class` (actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/http/request.rb:88) calls `"posts".camelize + "Controller"` then `constantize` to resolve `PostsController`.

`dispatch` calls `controller.dispatch(action, req, res)` — the class method on Metal.

---

## 4. ActionController::Metal — Class Method Dispatch

```
actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal.rb:331-337
```
```ruby
def self.dispatch(name, req, res)
  if middleware_stack.any?
    middleware_stack.build(name) { |env| new.dispatch(name, req, res) }.call req.env
  else
    new.dispatch(name, req, res)
  end
end
```

Controller-level middleware (e.g. `use AuthenticationMiddleware, except: [:index]`) wraps the instantiation here. Most controllers have an empty per-controller stack and take the `else` branch directly.

---

## 5. ActionController::Metal#dispatch — Instance Method

```
actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal.rb:249-255
```
```ruby
def dispatch(name, request, response)
  set_request!(request)    # stores @_request, sets req.controller_instance = self
  set_response!(response)  # stores @_response
  process(name)
  request.commit_flash      # writes flash changes back to session
  to_a                      # calls response.to_a → [status, headers, body]
end
```

## 6. AbstractController::Base#process

```
actionpack/lib/abstract_controller/base.rb:146-156
```
```ruby
def process(action, ...)
  @_action_name = action.to_s
  unless action_name = _find_action_name(@_action_name)
    raise ActionNotFound.new(...)
  end
  @_response_body = nil
  process_action(action_name, ...)
end
```

`_find_action_name` calls `method_for_action` which checks `action_methods` (the set of public instance methods not in `AbstractController::Base`). If no method exists but a template does, `ImplicitRender#method_for_action` (actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal/implicit_render.rb) returns `"default_render"`.

`process_action` now climbs the module chain. `ActionController::Base` includes modules in a specific order that determines the nesting — outermost first:

---

## 7. ActionController::Instrumentation#process_action (outermost wrapper)

```
actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal/instrumentation.rb:59-86
```
```ruby
def process_action(*)
  ActiveSupport::ExecutionContext[:controller] = self
  raw_payload = { controller:, action:, request:, params:, … }

  ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("start_processing.action_controller", raw_payload)

  ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("process_action.action_controller", raw_payload) do |payload|
    result = super                          # ← calls Rescue#process_action
    payload[:response] = response
    payload[:status]   = response.status
    result
  rescue => error
    payload[:status] = ExceptionWrapper.status_code_for_exception(error.class.name)
    raise
  ensure
    append_info_to_payload(payload)        # appends view_runtime, db_runtime
  end
end
```

Two `ActiveSupport::Notifications` events bracket the entire action. LogSubscriber listens to `process_action.action_controller` to emit the `Completed 200 OK` log line.

---

## 8. ActionController::Rescue#process_action

```
actionpack/lib/action_controller/metal/rescue.rb:35-40
```
```ruby
def process_action(*)
  super                                              # ← calls Callbacks#process_action
rescue Exception => exception
  request.env["action_dispatch.show_detailed_exceptions"] ||= show_detailed_exceptions?
  rescue_with_handler(exception) || raise
end
```

`rescue_with_handler` (from `ActiveSupport::Rescuable`) walks the `rescue_handlers` chain registered via `rescue_from`, matching by class ancestry.

## 9. AbstractController::Callbacks#process_action

```
actionpack/lib/abstract_controller/callbacks.rb:34-39  (setup)
```
```ruby
included do
  define_callbacks :process_action,
    terminator: ->(controller, result_lambda) { result_lambda.call; controller.performed? },
    skip_after_callbacks_if_terminated: true
end
```

The override (abstract_controller/callbacks.rb:~265):
```ruby
def process_action(...)
  run_callbacks(:process_action) { super }   # super → AbstractController::Base#process_action
end
```

`ActiveSupport::Callbacks#run_callbacks` (activesupport/lib/active_support/callbacks.rb:97) compiles the callback chain at first use into a single method via `module_eval`. For each `before_action` filter it calls the filter then checks the terminator lambda — if `performed?` (meaning `render` or `redirect_to` was already called) the chain halts and after_actions are skipped.

`around_action` filters receive a block they must `yield`; if they don't yield, the chain is halted.

## 10. Action Method Execution

```
actionpack/lib/abstract_controller/base.rb:223-231
```
```ruby
def process_action(...)
  send_action(...)
end

alias send_action send
```

`send(action_name, ...)` invokes the controller method — e.g. `PostsController#index`. This is where user code runs.

If the action calls `render`, control flows immediately into the rendering pipeline. If it calls neither `render` nor `redirect_to`, `ImplicitRender#default_render` is called after the action returns (from `method_for_action` returning `"default_render"`).

## 12. ActionView::Rendering#render_to_body

```

```
```ruby
def render_to_body(options = {}, &block)
  _process_options(options)
  _process_render_template_options(options)
  _render_template(options, &block)
end

def _render_template(options, &block)
  variant = options.delete(:variant)
  assigns = options.delete(:assigns)
  context = view_context        # ActionView::Base.new(lookup_context, assigns, controller)

  context.assign assigns if assigns
  lookup_context.variants = variant if variant

  rendered_template = context.in_rendering_context(options) do |renderer|
    renderer.render_to_object(context, options, &block)
  end

  rendered_format = rendered_template.format || lookup_context.formats.first
  @_rendered_format = Template::Types[rendered_format]
  rendered_template.body
end
```

`view_context` (actionview/lib/action_view/rendering.rb:109) creates an instance of the controller's `view_context_class` — a dynamically-generated subclass of `ActionView::Base` that includes all helpers. The controller instance is passed in as `self` for helpers like `url_for`.

## 14. ActionView::TemplateRenderer

```
actionview/lib/action_view/renderer/template_renderer.rb:5-12
```
```ruby
def render(context, options, &block)
  @details = extract_details(options)          # format, locale, handlers, variants
  template = determine_template(options, &block)  # selects template object
  prepend_formats(template.format)
  render_template(context, template, options[:layout], options[:locals] || {})
end
```

`determine_template` (template_renderer.rb:16-54) dispatches on `options` keys: `:body`/`:plain`/`:html`/`:inline`/`:template`/`:renderable`. For the typical `render :show` case it falls through to `@lookup_context.find_template(options[:template], prefixes, false, keys, @details)`.

```
actionview/lib/action_view/renderer/template_renderer.rb:58-83
```
```ruby
def render_template(view, template, layout_name, locals)
  render_with_layout(view, template, layout_name, locals) do |layout|
    ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("render_template.action_view", …) do
      template.render(view, locals) { |*name| view._layout_for(*name) }
    end
  end
end

def render_with_layout(view, template, path, locals)
  layout = path && find_layout(path, locals.keys, [formats.first])
  body = if layout
    ActiveSupport::Notifications.instrument("render_layout.action_view", …) do
      view.view_flow.set(:layout, yield(layout))        # render inner template
      layout.render(view, locals) { |*name| view._layout_for(*name) }  # render layout
    end
  else
    yield        # no layout — render template directly
  end
  build_rendered_template(body, template)
end
```

Layouts are found by `find_layout` → `resolve_layout` → `_find_layout` which looks in `lookup_context` for `"layouts/application"` (or whatever `layout` declared). The inner template is rendered first (yielded into the layout block), then the layout template is rendered with the inner content available via `yield` / `content_for`.

## 15. Template Lookup — ActionView::LookupContext

```
actionview/lib/action_view/lookup_context.rb
```

`LookupContext` holds an ordered list of `view_paths` (resolvers) and the current detail set (formats, locale, handlers, variants). `find_template` calls through to `@view_paths.find(name, prefixes, partial, details, details_key, keys)`.

`FileSystemResolver` (actionview/lib/action_view/template/resolver.rb:90) maintains a `Concurrent::Map` keyed on `TemplatePath.virtual(name, prefix, partial)`. On a miss it calls `unbound_templates_from_path` which globs the filesystem for files matching `"{prefix}/{name}{partial_marker}.{locale}.{handler}.{format}.{variant}"` patterns. Results are `UnboundTemplate` objects that are bound to the requested locals.

## 16. ActionView::Template#render — Compilation and Execution

```
actionview/lib/action_view/template.rb:280-299
```
```ruby
def render(view, locals, buffer = nil, implicit_locals: [], add_to_stack: true, &block)
  instrument_render_template do
    compile!(view)
    # … strict locals filtering …
    result = view._run(method_name, self, locals, OutputBuffer.new, …, &block)
    result.is_a?(OutputBuffer) ? result.to_s : result
  end
rescue => e
  handle_render_error(view, e)
end
```

**`compile!`** (template.rb:433-453) is thread-safe via `@compile_mutex`:
```ruby
def compile!(view)
  return if @compiled
  @compile_mutex.synchronize do
    return if @compiled
    mod = view.compiled_method_container    # the view context's anonymous module
    instrument("!compile_template") { compile(mod) }
    @compiled = true
  end
end
```

**`compile`** calls `compiled_source` (template.rb:458) which:
1. Calls `@handler.call(self, source)` — for ERB this is `ActionView::Template::Handlers::ERB` which runs `Erubi::Engine` to turn `<% ... %>` into Ruby string interpolations
2. Wraps the output in a method definition:
   ```ruby
   def _app_views_posts_show_html_erb_HASH(local_assigns, output_buffer)
     @virtual_path = "posts/show"; ...; <compiled ERB body>
   end
   ```
3. Calls `mod.module_eval(source, identifier, offset)` to define that method on the view context's module

**`view._run`** (actionview/lib/action_view/base.rb:264) calls `public_send(method_name, locals, buffer)` — invoking the compiled ERB method.

---

## 17. Response Assembly and Rack Return

After `ActionView::Template#render` returns a string (or `OutputBuffer#to_s`), control returns up through:

- `TemplateRenderer#render_with_layout` → `build_rendered_template(body, template)` → `RenderedTemplate` struct
- `ActionView::Rendering#_render_template` → `rendered_template.body` → String
- `AbstractController::Rendering#render` → `self.response_body = rendered_body`
- `ActionController::Metal#response_body=` → `response.body = body`
- `ActionDispatch::Response#body=` (response.rb:385) wraps string into `Buffer` at `@stream`

When `Metal#dispatch` finishes `process(name)`, it calls `to_a` (metal.rb:254) → `response.to_a`:

```
actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/http/response.rb:461-464
```
```ruby
def to_a
  commit!
  rack_response @status, @headers.to_hash
end
```

`commit!` triggers `before_committed` (sets Content-Type defaults, ETag, finalizes cache headers) and `before_sending` which calls **`request.commit_cookie_jar!`** — this serializes session and cookies back into `Set-Cookie` headers.

`rack_response` returns `[status, headers, RackBody.new(self)]` where `RackBody#each` yields the stream to the Rack server.

The `[status, headers, body]` triplet bubbles back up through all 30 middleware layers in reverse. Each middleware can inspect or transform the response (e.g. `Rack::ETag` adds/validates `ETag`, `Rack::ConditionalGet` rewrites 200→304 on match, `ActionDispatch::Cookies` was already handled via `before_sending`, `ActionDispatch::Executor` closes the body in a `Rack::BodyProxy` to return the database connection after the last byte is sent).

## Key Cross-Cutting Observations

**The `env` hash is the universal bus.** Every subsystem communicates through it: `ActionDispatch::RequestId` stamps `"action_dispatch.request_id"`, `CookieStore` puts decrypted session in `"rack.session"`, `RemoteIp` puts the trusted IP in `"action_dispatch.remote_ip"`, the router deposits `"action_dispatch.route"` and `path_parameters`, the controller registers itself at `"action_controller.instance"`.

**`process_action` is a middleware chain within the controller.** `Instrumentation` wraps `Rescue` wraps `Callbacks`. Each calls `super` to pass control inward, exactly mirroring the outer Rack middleware pattern.

**Template compilation is one-time and module-scoped.** `Template#compile!` runs once per template per process (or reloading cycle). The compiled method is `module_eval`'d onto the view context's anonymous module, so every subsequent render for that template is a plain Ruby method call — no string interpolation at runtime.

**Connections are held for the minimum duration.** `ActionDispatch::Executor` wraps the request in `executor.run!(reset: true)` and uses `Rack::BodyProxy` to defer `executor.complete!` until the response body is fully consumed — returning the ActiveRecord connection to the pool only after streaming is done.
